Links: [{"title":"github-mcp-server/docs/installation-guides/install-claude.md at main · github/github-mcp-server","url":"https://github.com/github/github-mcp-server/blob/main/docs/installation-guides/install-claude.md"},{"title":"Connect Claude Code to tools via MCP - Claude Code Docs","url":"https://code.claude.com/docs/en/mcp"},{"title":"Configuring MCP Tools in Claude Code - The Better Way - Scott Spence","url":"https://scottspence.com/posts/configuring-mcp-tools-in-claude-code"},{"title":"Add MCP Servers to Claude Code - Setup & Configuration Guide | MCPcat","url":"https://mcpcat.io/guides/adding-an-mcp-server-to-claude-code/"},{"title":"Ultimate Guide to Claude MCP Servers & Setup | 2026","url":"https://generect.com/blog/claude-mcp/"},{"title":"Connect to local MCP servers - Model Context Protocol","url":"https://modelcontextprotocol.io/docs/develop/connect-local-servers"},{"title":"Connect Claude to an MCP Server | Generate SDKs for your API with liblab","url":"https://liblab.com/docs/mcp/howto-connect-mcp-to-claude"},{"title":"Add MCP Servers to Claude Code with MCP Toolkit | Docker","url":"https://www.docker.com/blog/add-mcp-servers-to-claude-code-with-mcp-toolkit/"},{"title":"docs/ci-autopilot/overview/use-mcp-server/configuration/claude-code-cli.md at main · trunk-io/docs","url":"https://github.com/trunk-io/docs/blob/main/ci-autopilot/overview/use-mcp-server/configuration/claude-code-cli.md"},{"title":"Set Up MCP with Claude Code | SailPoint Developer Community","url":"https://developer.sailpoint.com/docs/extensibility/mcp/integrations/claude-code/"}]

Based on the search results, here's current information about Claude Code CLI MCP server configuration:

## Configuration Methods

MCP servers can be configured in three different ways depending on your needs, with HTTP servers being the recommended option for connecting to remote MCP servers as the most widely supported transport for cloud-based services.

## CLI Configuration Commands

You can add MCP servers to Claude Code with the command `claude mcp add`, and common commands include: Add server with `claude mcp add [name] --scope user`, List servers with `claude mcp list`, and Remove server with `claude mcp remove [name]`.(no content)The basic syntax for HTTP servers is `claude mcp add --transport http <name> <url>`

## Direct Configuration File Editing

For complex configurations, you can skip the CLI wizard and edit the config file directly at ~/Library/Application Support/Claude/claude_desktop_config.json.(no content)Directly editing the config file gives much more control and flexibility, especially for anyone with more complex setups or testing locally, allowing you to quickly set up, modify, and maintain MCP tool configurations without the frustration of stepping through CLI prompts.

## Authentication & Scopes

The --scope flag is optional and can specify where the configuration is stored: local (default) - available only to you in the current project, project - shared with everyone via .mcp.json file, or user - available to you across all projects.

## Output Management

When MCP tools produce large outputs, Claude Code displays a warning when any MCP tool output exceeds 10,000 tokens, with a default maximum limit of 25,000 tokens that can be adjusted using the MAX_MCP_OUTPUT_TOKENS environment variable.


REMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks.

content.text:
Based on the search results I found, here's how to configure MCP servers in Claude Code CLI:

## Configuration File Location

The MCP server configuration file is located at:
- **macOS**: `~/Library/Application Support/Claude/claude_desktop_config.json`
- **Windows**: `%APPDATA%\Claude\claude_desktop_config.json`
- **Linux**: `~/.config/Claude/claude_desktop_config.json`

## Configuration Methods

### 1. Using CLI Commands (Recommended for Simple Setup)

```bash
# Add a new MCP server
claude mcp add [name] --scope user

# List configured servers
claude mcp list

# Remove a server
claude mcp remove [name]
```

### 2. Direct Configuration File Editing

You can edit the `claude_desktop_config.json` file directly for more control. The configuration format supports multiple transport types (HTTP, stdio, SSE, etc.).

### 3. Scope Options

When adding a server, you can specify the scope using the `--scope` flag:
- **local** (default): Available only in the current project
- **project**: Shared with everyone via `.mcp.json` file in your project
- **user**: Available across all your projects

## Example Configuration

For HTTP-based MCP servers, the basic command syntax is:
```bash
claude mcp add --transport http <name> <url>
```

## Configuration Storage

You can also use a `.mcp.json` file in your project directory for project-specific configurations that can be shared with your team.

## Output Management

Claude Code displays a warning when MCP tool output exceeds 10,000 tokens. The default maximum is 25,000 tokens, which can be adjusted using the `MAX_MCP_OUTPUT_TOKENS` environment variable.

For the most up-to-date and comprehensive documentation, I recommend checking the official Claude Code documentation at https://code.claude.com/docs/en/mcp, which covers all transport types, authentication options, and advanced configurations.

Sources:
- [Connect Claude Code to tools via MCP - Claude Code Docs](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/mcp)
- [github-mcp-server installation guide](https://github.com/github/github-mcp-server/blob/main/docs/installation-guides/install-claude.md)
- [Ultimate Guide to Claude MCP Servers & Setup 2026](https://generect.com/blog/claude-mcp/)
